claims.claim: Illmari Vaino is the founder of Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Gin-Soaked Rag is associated with the Sin and Tonic.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Dura Oil Drinker is a mechanic associated with Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Elias serves as the quartermaster of Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: The Roundhack gang is run by Tywelwyn Leatherhide.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Tywelwyn Leatherhide ordered materials from Embercall Industries, which is associated with the Pyrelight faction.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The faction known as Pyrelight operates a front company called Embercall Industries.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The local law enforcement in the area is called the Threshold Authority, also known as the TA.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Threshold Authority is suspected of being bought out by either the Roundhack gang or Embercall Industries/Pyrelight.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Augers are creatures created through a divination ritual and can be fed to enhance divination magic.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: The Sin and Tonic serves as the base of operations for Iconoclasm.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Iconoclasm possesses a company car available for use by its members.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Ginny's appearance consists of a suit vest and dress pants, but instead of a head, Ginny has a twisted-up dish rag.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Ginny works the bar and can be found facing away from approaching patrons when tending it.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Ginny's rag-head appears to squeeze out liquid when it twists to face someone.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Illmari is normally responsible for cooking food at the establishment.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Ginny works the cooking station in addition to tending bar, at least when Illmari is absent.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Ginny has not mastered cooking human food as well as Illmari has.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The twins are not running a magic item shop, but are two people who are particularly good at crafting and can make healing potions if provided with the necessary components.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The NPC crafter works in a room where rocket hammers are made.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The radar dish is believed to be a device for receiving a signal.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anok keeps a Rolodex-like collection of index cards containing contacts and job leads, which she uses to refer people to work opportunities.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anok has an old friend who runs a scrapyard located one district over from her location in Sableclutch.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anok's contact at the scrapyard in Sableclutch regularly hires couriers and similar workers, preferring to engage individuals directly rather than through a formal service.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Arbiters are empowered by the Scale to conduct bounty hunting, and the Scale does not extend that authority to unaffiliated individuals.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Three police organizations operate in the city of Helya, and each typically posts bounties for outstanding warrants that non-Scale individuals can pursue.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In Hallia, particularly strong magic set up by the host prevents people from teleporting or sliding between districts freely; residents must use public transit to travel between areas.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Hallia has a trolley system that travels along a route called the Horizon, connecting different numbered districts such as Star 12.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The neighborhood containing the Sin and Tonic is a commercialized, generally poorer part of Helya, but not particularly seedy.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The starward districts of Helya feature older, poorly maintained buildings that were constructed when the tram line was first built, in anticipation of a boom that never materialized.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Anzu has a raven companion named Othello.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Undertable is a narrow, squished shop wedged between two buildings in a space too large for an alleyway but too small for a standard building, with no street-facing windows and a handwritten sign reading 'Under Table' accompanied by a crude drawing of a table.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Undertable's interior is a cramped room filled with tables stacked high with miscellaneous goods including kitchen appliances, loose jewelry, radios in various states of deconstruction, and a large number of firearms.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler is a centaur and the proprietor of the Undertable, as well as a fence known to Johnny.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler wears a stained white t-shirt as his only upper garment.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Johnny has a prior working relationship with Smiler and has dealt with him as a fence on at least one or two previous occasions.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler stands approximately nine feet tall at full centaur height, and must stoop beneath the ceiling of the Undertable.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler wears multiple yellowish metal bands on both arms, has his tail braided with gems woven into it, wears flare gauges in his ears with gems embedded in them, and has a long beard braided in the same style as his tail with gems throughout.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Smiler extends a friends discount to associates of Johnny, on the basis that a friend of his friend would not inform on him to law enforcement.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Belvedere includes a school and provides education to children.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Belvedere is located hundreds of miles away from the region where the party is operating.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The country of Raelion was destroyed (referred to as 'gone'), an event that displaced its inhabitants.
claims.speaker: Johnny

claims.claim: Sableclutch is a neighborhood located near the party's base of operations.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a scrapyard located in or near the Sableclutch neighborhood.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Hallia has a public tram transportation system.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch is not a prosperous part of town.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch contains a notable concentration of religious buildings, including numerous churches.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The churches in Sableclutch are mostly dedicated to the Host, with a smattering of other gods also represented.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The scrapyard is located on the voidward side of Hallia, reachable by tram.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The voidward strata of Hallia contains slums between the city center and the scrapyard.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The voidward direction of Hallia is characterized by undeveloped land and feels like the far outskirts despite being geographically close to the city center.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The voidward part of Hallia is home primarily to factories that dump waste into the river running through Hallia, situated there to avoid legal consequences or harming residents.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A river runs through the city of Hallia.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a scrapyard located in the voidward industrial district of Hallia, notable for its expansive grounds and mountains of trash.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is a person known to Anouk who operates at or is associated with Sableclutch Scrap.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is a dwarven man who operates a Crusher.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw has a mashed-up, misshapen nose and a black eye, indicating he still gets into fights regularly.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw appears to be at the end of middle age or beginning of elderly by dwarven standards, estimated to be around 120 to 130 years old.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: By dwarven reckoning, the end of middle age corresponds to approximately 120 to 130 years old.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw's bottom jaw is missing and has been replaced with a crude copper prosthetic vaguely shaped like a jaw, which is the origin of his name.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is the kind of person who tests the grip strength of anyone who shakes his hand, regardless of their age, and responds competitively to a strong handshake.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The employer's policy is that couriers are not told what is inside the suitcase; they are only expected to deliver it.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw operates a scrapyard.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw has a Giant Crusher stationed at his scrapyard, which he relies on for security when dealing with unwanted visitors.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Cars exist in this setting, and as a consequence tire companies also exist.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Korath is a major tire company in this setting, and it publishes a guide known as the Korath Guide.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Diamond District is a relatively upscale part of the city, known for jewelers and fine metal shops, and is located near high-end residential areas.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Cobblejaw operates out of a junkyard where he crafts items for clients.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Cobblejaw personally owns and keeps three guns for himself.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The setting does not have television; radio shows are the primary broadcast entertainment medium.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Tarv is a comic-book character known for having a gun for an arm, and the concept exists across multiple spheres or worlds through convergent cultural evolution.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The setting is a magical society in which bird people and similar non-human beings are common enough that they do not attract unusual attention in public.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Threshold Authority officers wear a greenish uniform with a large 'TA' insignia on it.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline is a law enforcement organization that generally polices the nicer parts of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline officers wear full uniforms and carry batons as their primary weapons rather than firearms.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline operates on a subscription model, providing law enforcement services only to those who pay for them.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Sableclutch Scrap is a junkyard whose owner, Coppajaw, is known to give jobs to newcomers.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline officers carry badges with identification numbers.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Blackline is a private law enforcement company that operates in at least some districts of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Copperjaw is known to hire couriers or runners to deliver important items, sometimes using children carrying cases.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Officer Smith is a Blackline officer who patrols the district; he carries a badge with an identification number.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: One of the two Blackline officers encountered is mute due to a bad accident sustained in a scuffle with a local gang; his colleagues call him Mute.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: A trolley station is accessible from the factory and scrapyard district the party was moving through.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area the party is traveling through has relatively winding streets.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area lacks traffic cops because employing them would cost money, and there is no clear party to fund them.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The area contains factories and scrapyards, which are serviced by trucks carting goods in and out.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram station serves workers heading home from nearby factories, with goblinoid laborers among the regular commuters.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: Goblinoid creatures work in the factories in the area near the tram station.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The Masque District is a part of the city that contains hotels and is a destination for tourists.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram system has named stations, including at least 'Void 2' and 'Zero' (also called 'Prime'), suggesting a numbered or tiered station naming convention.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The tram line in this city is operated by Prime Meridian.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The residential district the party is heading to is wealthy enough that all its inhabitants can afford private cars, making public transport unavailable into that part of the city.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: One of the two figures, when opening its mouth to laugh, produced a weird gargling sound instead of normal laughter.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: The two figures are shapeshifters capable of growing approximately a foot and a half in size and shedding their humanoid disguises to reveal their true forms.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In their true form, the two creatures appear as large, pink, fleshy, naked mole rat-like beings, with their clothes melting into their bodies during transformation.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster

claims.claim: In their true form, the creatures' eyes sink into their heads leaving only empty sockets, and most other facial features melt back into the skin, leaving them nearly featureless.
claims.speaker: Gamemaster
